topics.label: Potions
topics.recapText: You know my stock. Healing potions and the... other kind.

topics.label: Rare Ingredients
topics.recapText: Moonpetal, drake scale, frost crystal: the three pillars of real alchemy. I already told you about them.

greeting.speakerText: Yes, yes, come in! Mind the fumes. And the bubbling. And whatever that purple stain on the ceiling is. What do you need?

return_greeting.speakerText: You again! Excellent. I was just about to test something on a living subject. I mean: welcome back! What can I do for you?

potions_greeting.speakerText: Potions! My pride and joy. I've got remedies that'll knit flesh back together and... other concoctions for when flesh needs to come apart. What interests you?

responses.displayText: Tell me about your healing potions.
Those prices seem a bit steep, don't you think?

potions_healing_response.speakerText: Standard red vials: mend cuts, soothe burns, reattach the occasional finger. Three gold each. I guarantee results or your money back. Well, most of your money. Some of it. Don't hold me to that.

potions_poisons_response.speakerText: Ah, a discerning customer! I have nightbloom extract for the subtle approach and stonewyrm bile for when subtlety isn't the point. Five gold each, and I never saw you here.

potions_haggle_pass.speakerText: You drive a hard bargain! Fine: two gold for healing, four for the poisons. But only because I like the cut of your jib. Whatever a jib is.

potions_haggle_fail.speakerText: Steep? STEEP? Do you have any idea what moonpetal costs these days? My prices are practically charity. Full price or nothing!

potions_buy_response.speakerText: A customer who appreciates fair pricing! Wonderful. Come back anytime: I always have stock.

experiment_greeting.speakerText: Listen, I'm on the verge of something extraordinary. A transmutation formula that could change the very nature of matter itself! But I need an extra pair of hands. Preferably ones still attached to a living person.

responses.displayText: What kind of experiment are we talking about?
I'll help you with the experiment.

experiment_details_response.speakerText: I've been trying to stabilize volatile quintessence: the fifth elemental extract. Every alchemist who's attempted it has either blown up their laboratory or turned themselves into a newt. I'm confident I can do better. Fairly confident. Somewhat confident.

experiment_help_dialogue.speakerText: Brilliant! Absolutely brilliant! First, I need you to gather some reagents. Come back when you're ready and we'll begin phase two. Try not to die before then: I hate wasting good assistants.

experiment_phase2.speakerText: You're back! Perfect timing: the quintessence is reaching critical resonance. I can hear it humming. Or that might be the wasps in the walls. Either way, we need to act fast!

responses.displayText: What's the current state of the quintessence?

experiment_p2_status_response.speakerText: It's oscillating between a deep violet and a concerning shade of angry red. The textbooks say violet is 'ready' and red is 'run.' We're somewhere in between. Exciting, isn't it?

experiment_p2_proceed_dialogue.speakerText: We did it! The quintessence is stable! Well, stable-ish. Stable enough. You have a genuine gift for not panicking when things catch fire. I won't forget this!

ingredients_greeting.speakerText: Rare ingredients are the lifeblood of real alchemy. Any fool can boil mushrooms and call it a potion. True craft requires truly extraordinary components. I'm always hunting for three in particular.

responses.displayText: What's so special about drake scales?

ingredients_moonpetal_response.speakerText: Moonpetal only blooms under a full moon in high-altitude meadows. The petals absorb lunar energy: sounds mystical, but the biochemistry is quite real. One petal can amplify a healing potion's potency tenfold. I'd pay twenty gold for a fresh specimen.

ingredients_drake_scale_response.speakerText: Drake scales contain a catalytic enzyme that no artificial process can replicate. The problem is, you have to get them from a drake. And drakes are, shall we say, uncooperative about the whole thing. There are rumors of a dragon nesting in the eastern mountains: where there are dragons, there are drakes.

ingredients_frost_crystal_response.speakerText: Frost crystals form deep in glacial caves where the temperature never rises above freezing. They're nature's perfect preservative: a single shard can keep a potion stable for decades. The trick is transporting them without letting them melt. I've lost three shipments this year alone.

dragon_rumors_greeting.speakerText: Ah, so you've been thinking about what I said regarding the drakes. The eastern mountains have been producing strange smoke plumes for weeks. That's no forest fire: that's dragonfire. Something big has taken up residence there.

responses.displayText: What else have you heard about this dragon?
There must be more to this. What aren't people saying?

dragon_rumors_details_response.speakerText: Miners from the foothills say they've heard wingbeats at night. A merchant claimed he saw a shadow blot out the stars: something with a wingspan wider than a galleon. Whether it's one dragon or several, I couldn't say. But the drake scale supply has dried up completely. They've either fled or been eaten.

dragon_rumors_investigate_pass.speakerText: Sharp mind you've got! Yes: there's a pattern. The smoke plumes appear every three days, always at dusk, always from the same peak. That's not random hunting: that's a nest. A dragon guarding eggs. If you time your approach between the plumes, you might reach the outer caves while it's dormant. That's where the drakes would shelter.

dragon_rumors_investigate_fail.speakerText: I wish I knew more, but that's all I've got. Smoke, shadows, and secondhand stories. If you learn anything concrete, I'd pay well for that information.
